When he took office in 1817, President James Monroe's first choice for Secretary of War was Henry Clay, who declined the position. The post ultimately went to Calhoun. The result was that Johnson became chair of the Committee on Expenditures where he wielded considerable influence over defense policy in the Department of War during the Fifteenth Congress. In 1817, Congress investigated General Andrew Jackson's execution of two British subjects during the First Seminole War. Johnson chaired the inquiry committee. The majority of the committee favored a negative report and a censure for Jackson. Johnson, a Jackson supporter, drafted a minority report that was more favorable to Jackson and opposed the censure. The ensuing debate pitted Johnson against fellow Kentuckian Clay. Johnson's report prevailed, and Jackson was spared censure. This disagreement between Johnson and Clay, however, marked the beginning of a political separation between the two that lasted for the duration of their careers.

Korovkin is described as “a brilliant and ferocious poet whose furious, defiant but consistently classical and supremely elegant poetry, controlled and yet fluid, draws, without ever becoming heavy, on a very rich and varied cultural patrimony” (Franca Rovigatti, Roma Poesia 1998). It consists of 14 poems whose programmatic direction is best attested to by the title of the first piece of the volume: Looking for Faith. The economy of Jemari (J.K. Nagar Township) is closely tied to the industrial legacy of Lakshmipat Singhania, who moved to Kolkata in 1942. He established the Aluminium Corporation of India near Asansol, India's first aluminium producer, which began operations in 1944. The company played a pivotal role in India's industrial growth, with its own coal mine, power plant, and manufacturing facilities. However, the 1960s saw militant trade unionism and political interference disrupt productivity, culminating in an incident where workers brutally assaulted the works manager for over two hours at the factory gate. In 1984, the Aluminium Corporation of India, Asansol, was merged with Bharat Aluminium Company (BALCO), becoming the BALCO-Bidhanbag unit. BALCO, India's first public sector aluminium company (established in 1965), was later privatized in 2001 when the government sold a 51% stake to Sterlite Industries (now Vedanta Limited).

Stoner Witch was inducted into Spin's Hall of Fame as one of 'The 20 Greatest Grunge Albums of All Time'. The band also ranked among 'The 30 Best Grunge Albums' on Treblezine and featured as one of '10 Essential Stoner Rock Albums' on the same publication.

The movement to create Inns of Court in the United States was led by U.S. Chief Justice Warren E. Burger from the late 1970s, and it was loosely modeled after the traditional English Inns of Court. The American Inns of Court Foundation was established in 1985 by Burger and others to support this initiative. Each local Inn of Court serves to foster a supportive community for legal professionals, focusing on promoting ethics, civility, and professionalism among the American bench and bar. Major American cities typically host multiple Inns of Court, often named after renowned legal figures. For example, the Atlas IP Inn in Houston includes judges, lawyers, law professors, and law students. American Inns of Court do not possess any real property; instead, they are comprised of members who meet regularly—typically once a month—to engage in discussions and programs centered on legal ethics and skills. Meetings often include a shared meal and presentations by pupillage teams, facilitating mentorship and ongoing education within the legal community.

Murray wore their hair short and preferred pants to skirts; and due to their slight build, there was a time in their life when they were often able to pass as a teenage boy. In their twenties, they shortened their name from Pauline to the more androgynous Pauli. At the time of their arrest for the bus segregation protest in 1940, they gave their name as "Oliver" to the arresting officers. Murray pursued hormone treatments in the 1940s to correct what they saw as a personal imbalance and even requested abdominal surgery to test if they had "submerged" male sex organs.

Early in this section Heilmann embarks on a thorough description of the Berlin Archaeopteryx specimen, which includes detailed comparisons to specific aspects of modern birds. Following a comparison of its skull to that of Aetosaurus, Euparkeria, and a modern pigeon, Heilmann states that he disagrees with the scientific consensus of the time that the skull of Archaeopteryx is that of a true bird. He writes that the reptilian features of the skull are much more pronounced, citing features of the teeth, fenestrae, and jaw structure as being undeniably un-birdlike. Heilmann found that much of Archaeopteryx's anatomy, in fact, was decidedly reptilian and generally opposed to that of modern birds. This included the pelvis, which lacks a pectineal process and has a very different os sacrum from modern birds, as well as the carpus, which Heilmann wrote exhibited the same phenomena found in the wrists of modern reptiles. Its tail was also noted by Heilmann as being extremely reptilian and resembling nothing known of modern birds.

Working uncredited at Harvey Comics for much of that time, Colón met editor Sid Jacobson, who became his frequent creative partner. Colón' earliest attributed work appears in two comics each cover-dated December 1960: the 15-page story "Spellbound" in Harvey's The Friendly Ghost, Casper #28, and, in a one-off for Archie Comics, the two-page featurette "Madhouse Stamps for Teens" in Archie's Madhouse #9. During Harvey's heyday in the 1960s and 1970s, Colón contributed anonymously to the company's many kid comics, estimating he drew about 15,000 pages. In April 1967, he drew and possibly wrote the two-page story for Wham-O Giant Comics #1, published by Wham-O. Between July 1968 and 1969, Colón penciled three issues of Gold Key Comics' *Doctor Solar, Man of the Atom* (#24-26). He also contributed to Warren Publishing's *Creepy*, *Eerie*, and *Vampirella*. For Skywald Publications' *Psycho* #3 (July 1971), Colón drew and lettered a story under the pen name "Willie Ito".

Jesse Woodson James (September 5, 1847 – April 3, 1882) was an American outlaw and leader of the James-Younger Gang, known for bank and train robberies in Missouri. A controversial figure, he became famous during his lifetime and later a legendary figure of the Wild West. Historians have variously interpreted his actions: T. J. Stiles contextualized him within post-Civil War Confederate insurgencies, while others viewed him as either a symbol of frontier lawlessness or a social bandit.

President Kennedy began seeking a diplomatic solution at a June 1961 meeting in Vienna. Soviet Premier Nikita Khrushchev both supported a neutral and independent Laos through a joint statement. As this meeting took place negotiators in Geneva got together to work out a settlement to the problem.

CLV applies the concept of present value to cash flows attributed to the customer relationship. Because the present value of any stream of future cash flows is designed to measure the single lump sum value today of the future stream of cash flows, CLV will represent the single lump sum value today of the customer relationship. CLV represents the monetary value of a customer relationship to the firm, serving as both the upper limit on what the firm would be willing to pay to acquire or retain the customer. Viewing a customer relationship as an asset, CLV presents its dollar value, reflecting the firm’s investment in and expectation from the customer over their lifetime.

During the first part of the intermediate ballistics period the real velocity of the projectile increases. It is caused by the propellant gases exiting the muzzle. For that reason the real maximum projectile velocity (Vmax) is higher than the real muzzle velocity (Vr). The external ballistics uses so-called initial velocity Vo, which is not the same as the real muzzle velocity. The initial velocity Vo is calculated via an extrapolation of the decaying part of velocity curve to the position of the muzzle (to). The difference between these two velocities is visible in the chart.

Chávez's successor, Nicolás Maduro was criticized for allowing corruption to remain in the government. In an article by The Washington Post, Venezuela's dire situation under Maduro was described as "a man-made disaster". The Bolivarian government was also seen as "a gangster state that doesn't know how to do anything other than sell drugs and steal money for itself" since Maduro's family members and close government officials were accused of being involved in illicit drug trade.

On 3 December 2018, Kjellberg announced that he had started a fundraiser on GoFundMe for Child Rights and You (CRY) to help Indian children, partially in response to racist comments left on his videos directed toward Indians. On 4 December, he hosted a livestream, donating the proceeds to CRY.

Most modern Christians consider these personas to be aspects of one deity, while traditional Christian theology takes the concept more literally. It typically holds that the three persons are homoousios, meaning they share the same essence or substance of divinity. Some suggest Christianity is a form of Tritheism.

The player interacts with characters and objects by clicking on them. Clicking on the ground causes the player's selected characters to move. The gameplay, though in real-time, can be paused, whereupon commands may be issued to controllable characters, who will attempt to execute them when the game is unpaused. The game can also be set to pause automatically at certain times. Dialogue is started by NPCs at certain scripted times, or by the player's clicking on NPCs who are not immediately hostile. When speaking to an NPC, the player must often choose what to say from a list of responses. The dialogue may lead to quests or important information. When the player clicks on a hostile being, the currently selected characters will advance to attack it. Information about characters, creatures, items, and buildings in the game environment is shown on a tool tip, which appears when the mouse pointer is held over game elements.

Erik Rauch (May 15, 1974 – July 13, 2005) was an American biophysicist and theoretical ecologist known for founding MetaCarta and contributing to spatial information processing systems. He earned degrees from Yale University, Stanford University, and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, and held positions at several prestigious institutions before his untimely death in a hiking accident.

Cao de Benós has been criticized for charging more than twice as much as other tour organizers to bring filmmakers, artists and tourists to North Korea. In exchange for higher prices, he promises exclusive access, though his itineraries offer comparable attractions. He has historically failed to secure permits for even standard, relatively accessible attractions.

The United States Naval Reserve Midshipmen's School at Smith College trained junior officers for the Women's Reserve of the U.S. Naval Reserve (WAVES), nicknamed "USS Northampton." On August 28, 1942, 120 women reported for training at the school.

The Finse Tunnel (Lang-no) is a 10,300-metre-long (6.4 mi; 11,300 yd) railway tunnel west of the village of Finse in Eidfjord Municipality in Vestland county, Norway. The tunnel is part of the Bergen Line between the cities of Oslo and Bergen. It is the longest tunnel on the line, and the third longest railway tunnel in the kingdom. Inside the tunnel, at 1,237 metres (4,058 ft) elevation, is the highest point on the Norwegian railway network.

The tunnel was constructed to increase the regularity of the line past the Finse peak which was often blocked by snow. Planning started during the 1980s to remove this section that was regularly snowed down, and at the same time reduce the length and increase the speed of the railway. Construction started in 1990, and it was opened by King Harald V on 16 May 1993. Following the construction of the tunnel, 32 kilometres (20 mi) of railway around this area was also rebuilt to achieve higher train speeds, a shorter overall distance, and to maximize the natural protection from the weather. This was built in five stages, opening between 1995 and 1998.

In some sections the road narrows down to 1 lane and others it turns very slippery. Local attractions include the forests, especially "Liquidambar" forest, especially when they change their leaves depending on the season. The coffee "Fincas" also provide scenic views.

Inspired by US pioneer Tracy Barnes' first manned solar balloon flight on 1 May 1973, Michaelis designed a free flight solar balloon. The design included a double envelope; the internal layer black polyester, while the outer was transparent. He hired Cameron Balloons in Bristol to build solar balloon G-BAVU. The solar balloon's envelopes worked in tandem: transparent surface allowed air to flow in, enabling a greenhouse effect and absorbed the trapped solar radiation from the black envelope. The twin envelopes were attached to a basket carrying a gas burner. The burner eased inflation and aided launch with cloudy skies. Altitude control was achieved through opening or closing horizontal panels located at the balloon equator. The deflation was made possible by rip-stop panels at the balloon crowns. From 1976 to 1980, his balloon (aircraft registration number G-BAVU) participated in a number of hot air balloon festivals in England.

Despite having been castigated as a myth of Cyril Hoskins' own creation, after more than four decades the book and its sequels remain the most popular publications claiming to represent Tibet. Worldwide, the book and its sequels continue to sell better than most other books about Tibet, despite there being no records of Rampa ever having lived, and despite the non-existence of a surgical procedure of drilling a third eye into the human forehead or other practises described in the book, such as out of body experiences on journeys to other planets in Tibetan Buddhism (sources?). Nonetheless, many readers, even those who understand it to be a work of fiction, continue to enjoy the book.

Nguyễn Thị Anh Thư (born April 26, 1982) is a Vietnamese supermodel and actor. She gained recognition in Vietnam with her role as Thuy in Long Legged Girls in 2004 and received further attention for lead roles in the television series Tropical Snow and Hoa thiên điểu. She was considered a prominent figure in the Vietnamese modeling industry from 2003 to 2012, often referred to as a leading model of that era.

Curaçao and Sint Maarten became autonomous countries ("landen") within the Kingdom of the Netherlands in 2010. They planned to introduce the Caribbean guilder as a joint currency, initially expected by 2024, but its launch was delayed to March 31, 2025. Unlike Aruba, which gained the right to secede, Curaçao and Sint Maarten were not granted this option.

The situation in France was now beyond remedy, and the British government decided to evacuate its remaining troops from the Atlantic ports between 15 and 17 June (Operation Aerial). Detachments of 73rd AA Rgt maintained temporary cover for 72 hours along the route of the evacuation, and two of its precious GL radar sets were got away from Saint-Nazaire by 79th (Hertfordshire Yeomanry) AA Rgt of 3 AA Bde, but the rest of the equipment had to be destroyed on the dockside. Half of 12 AA Bde HQ embarked at Nantes, the remainder went to La Rochelle where the last vehicles and two GL sets were destroyed.

The primary evidence for astral projection is subjective personal accounts. Geologist Robert Crookall collected hundreds of these accounts and published them in books to validate religious concepts. However, due to their subjective nature, Crookall's objectivity has been questioned.

Snatam Kaur's music is deeply influenced by Sikhism, a religion founded in the mid-15th century in India by Guru Nanak Dev Ji (1469-1539). As the first Guru of Sikhism, Guru Nanak's teachings emphasize meditating on God, engaging with scriptures, and contributing to the well-being of others. Sikhs strive to live according to these teachings. Kaur's teacher, Yogi Bhajan (1929-2004), played a significant role in promoting the Sikh tradition in the Western world.

In Hinduism, a murti (Devanagari: मूर्ति) (also spelled murthi or murthy) typically refers to an image in which the Divine Spirit is 'murta', or expressed. Hindus consider a murti worthy of worship after the divine is invoked in it for the purpose of offering worship. Thus the murti is regarded by Hindus and also by some Mahayana Buddhists during worship as points of devotional and meditational focus.

The 1952–53 FC Basel season was marked by significant success, as the team, managed by René Bader and Willy Dürr, won the Nationalliga A championship, finishing four points ahead of second-placed Young Boys. Basel played a total of 43 matches, achieving 27 wins, and reached the quarter-finals of the Swiss Cup. Josef Hügi was the top scorer, netting 32 league goals.

Tomita–Takesaki theory is a fundamental framework in the study of von Neumann algebras, focusing on the modular automorphisms of states. It introduces key concepts such as the modular operator and modular conjugation, which arise from the polar decomposition of certain antilinear operators associated with a cyclic and separating vector in a Hilbert space. The theory establishes a 1-parameter group of modular automorphisms linked to a state, revealing deep connections between different states and their automorphisms. Additionally, it explores KMS states and their characterization through modular automorphisms, contributing significantly to the understanding of the structure of type III factors in von Neumann algebras.
William Patrick Twomey Sr. (14 May 1899 - 25 March 1977) was an Australian rules footballer who played for Collingwood and Hawthorn in the Victorian Football League (VFL).

Also on 8 June, an Israeli missile allegedly targeted the Syrian capital of Damascus. The state news agency SANA reported that there were no casualties during the attack. The state news agency also maintained that the missile was intercepted, due to the activation of Syria's air defense system. According to the SOHR however, at least 11 pro-government fighters died during the attack. Many others were also injured, the Observatory added.
She is described as having long, straight black hair, brown eyes, and brown skin. Mo develops romantic feelings for the band's drummer, Charlie.

*Eastwood After Hours: Live at Carnegie Hall* is a live jazz album by Clint Eastwood and The Carnegie Hall Jazz Band, released on April 29, 1997, by Warner Bros. Records. The album was recorded on October 17, 1996, at Carnegie Hall in New York City, and produced by Phil Ramone, Clint Eastwood, and Bruce Ricker.

The album features songs from the soundtracks of Eastwood's films, performed by The Carnegie Hall Jazz Band, a string section, and jazz musicians such as Kenny Barron, Joshua Redman, and Roy Hargrove. The set included "Eastwood: After Hours Suite", featuring piano choruses played by Eastwood. The two-disc set includes archival photographs, film clips, and commentary. *Eastwood After Hours* received positive reviews, with critics praising it as a tribute to Eastwood's cultural legacy.

USS *Arizona* (1858) was a sidewheel steamer built in Wilmington, Delaware, for commercial use before being seized by the Confederacy during the American Civil War and renamed *Caroline* for blockade-running. Captured by the Union Navy in 1862, she was recommissioned as USS *Arizona* in 1863 and served in the West Gulf Blockading Squadron, participating in key operations such as the Battle of Grand Lake and the Red River Campaign. She also supported Union efforts at Port Hudson and Sabine Pass. The vessel was destroyed by fire in 1865 near New Orleans. Efforts to locate and preserve her wreck are ongoing through the USS Arizona Civil War Project Fund.

Sanjog is a 1972 Indian Hindi film directed by S. S. Balan, featuring Amitabh Bachchan and Mala Sinha. The plot follows Mohan and Asha, whose marriage faces familial opposition, leading to separation. Years later, they reunite under complicated circumstances, culminating in tragedy.
The Council on Religion and the Homosexual was a San Francisco-based organization founded in 1964 for the purpose of joining homosexual activists and religious leaders.

In the violinist scenario, the pregnant person was kidnapped: they did not consent to having the violinist plugged into them and they did nothing to cause the violinist to be plugged in, just as a person who is pregnant due to rape did nothing to cause the pregnancy. But in typical cases of abortion, the pregnant person had voluntary intercourse, and thus has either tacitly consented to allow the fetus to use their body (the tacit consent objection), or else has a duty to sustain the fetus because the pregnant person themself caused the fetus to stand in need of their body (the responsibility objection). Other common objections turn on the claim that the fetus is the pregnant person's child, whereas the violinist is a stranger (the stranger versus offspring objection).

Great Wishford is a village and civil parish in the Wylye Valley in Wiltshire, England, about three miles (5 km) north of Wilton and five miles (8 km) northwest of Salisbury. The village lies west of a bend in the River Wylye and has a triangular street layout comprising South Street, West Street and Station Road.

The carpet made its second appearance in public when it was displayed in 1985 at the Metropolitan Museum in New York. At a Sotheby's auction in Doha in 2009, an anonymous buyer bought it for $5.5 million. It is now a part of the permanent collection of the National Museum of Qatar.

Myrica cerifera is a small evergreen tree or large shrub native to North and Central America and the Caribbean. Its common names include southern wax myrtle, southern bayberry, candleberry, bayberry tree, and tallow shrub. It sees uses both in the garden and for candlemaking, as well as a medicinal plant.

Asceticism refers to spiritual disciplines practiced in some religious traditions, often associated with repentance and spiritual growth. While theological perspectives vary, some traditions view these practices as interacting with divine grace in the process of spiritual transformation and belief formation.
Louis of Brienne, also known as Louis of Acre (died in 1297), was viscount of Beaumont-sur-Sarthe in Maine, France.

"You Mean the World to Me" is the fifth single from Toni Braxton's critically and commercially successful debut album. Released in the spring of 1994, it was an instant radio hit, peaking at #7 on the Billboard Hot 100 and #3 on the Billboard Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Singles charts. The song describes Toni realizing her lover means the world to her, but he better shape up or ship out.

Born in San Jose, California, Coleman graduated from Lowell High School, then spent his entire playing career with the New York Yankees. He played six years in the Yankees' minor league system before reaching the big club in 1949. Coleman hit .275 in his first year and led all second basemen in fielding percentage. He was the Associated Press rookie of the year in 1949 and finished third in balloting by Baseball Writers' Association of America.

Every 300 years, the world of *Romancing SaGa 3* is threatened by the Rise of Morastrum, a cataclysmic event where a dark star eclipses the sun, causing all newborns to perish. However, a single child defies this fate, becoming known as the Child of Destiny. Six hundred years before the game's events, one such child survived and embraced darkness, becoming the Archfiend, an embodiment of evil. Three centuries later, another child emerged, resisting the Archfiend's influence and becoming the Matriarch, a figure of good. Nearly two decades have passed since the last Rise of Morastrum, and over 300 years since the Matriarch's appearance, and another Rise of Morastrum is imminent. The game begins with eight distinct characters, each with their own unique prologue and perspective, embarking on separate journeys that eventually converge as they investigate the potential emergence of a new Child of Destiny.

In 1963 Lieutenant William Szili offered his account of the shooting. He was the executive officer of a Company of Marines guarding the western boundary of the base. According to Szili, he and his company commander, Captain Arthur J. Jackson, who was awarded a Medal of Honor during World War II, had consumed approximately six martini cocktails at the base officer's club, on September 30, 1961. He said he left Jackson at the officer's club, went home to sleep, and was woken by a call from one of the base's provost officers, that Jackson had found Lopez in a "restricted area", and that Jackson needed his help.

It was announced that the Specials were to play the first day of the 2010 Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival in Coachella, California. Several weeks later it was announced that the band was to kick off a small US tour on Late Night with Jimmy Fallon on 13 April. The band hopes to return later in the year for a full US tour. They announced on their website on 1 February 2010 that they were going to play a show in Los Angeles the day before their performance in Coachella and also a show in Toronto, Canada.

In December 1914, Whitby Abbey was shelled by the German battlecruisers Von der Tann and Derfflinger, whose crew "were aiming for the Coastguard Station on the end of the headland." Scarborough and Hartlepool were also attacked. The abbey buildings sustained considerable damage during the ten-minute attack.

Of the built up area, industrial buildings made up 1.1% of the total area while housing and buildings made up 53.7% and transportation infrastructure made up 9.5%. while parks, green belts and sports fields made up 7.2%. Binningen's geography shows 40% heavily forested, 10% with orchards or small clusters of trees, 35% used for crops, 25% as pastures, and 5% for orchards or vine crops.

Booker's legacy has been debated within the civil rights community, where he was a significant figure. While criticized posthumously for his accommodationist approach to white supremacy, some scholars note his long-term goal of ending African American disenfranchisement. Since the late 20th century, assessments of his work have become more nuanced, with recent studies (as of 2010) acknowledging his accomplishments and leadership while recognizing the complexity of his strategies.

Lorita Waynesboro was born to parents Wayne Bernice Waynesboro and Minnie Peters. Prior to her settlement in New York, school and marriage records place Moss in Charleston, West Virginia and Lawrence, Ohio. She graduated with honors in 1936 from Garnet High School in Charleston, and played Mendelssohn's "War March of The Priests" on piano at the convocation. She had two sisters, Elnore and Frances, three and seven years younger respectively.

During the al-Aqsa Intifada, Israel conducted targeted killings of individuals it identified as involved in attacks against Israeli civilians. These individuals were reportedly members of militant groups including Fatah, Hamas, and Islamic Jihad, with some simultaneously holding positions in Palestinian Authority security forces. Israel justified these actions as necessary due to the Palestinian Authority's inability or unwillingness to arrest or extradite militants, citing both the degradation of Palestinian security infrastructure from Israeli military operations and potential risks of Palestinian civil conflict. Palestinian sources characterized these operations as state terrorism, citing civilian casualties and arguing that similar actions against Israeli leaders would be universally condemned as terrorism. Israel maintained that operations were carefully planned to minimize collateral damage and were responses to imminent threats, while asserting that Palestinian leadership had failed to meet counterterrorism obligations under the Oslo Accords.

On June 12, 2006, the National Directorate of Security (NDS), Afghanistan's national intelligence agency, issued a list of broadcasting and publishing activities that “must be banned” in light of heightened security problems that could deteriorate public morale. The list attributed negative intention, causality, and morality to reporting on terrorism and the Taliban insurgency. President Hamid Karzai denied these were instructions, claiming they were guidelines requesting media cooperation. Restricted activities included publishing exaggerated reports against national unity or peace, statements from armed groups, and leading with terrorism-related news. The guidelines aimed to prevent security issues and maintain public morale, though critics viewed them as limiting press freedom under the guise of national stability.

By 2016 the PMI was down for the fifth month indicating a cooling in manufacturing in China. Manufacturing activity is a key sign of economic performance. December was the tenth month in a row that manufacturing in China had contracted raising concerns that China's economy was not on steadier footing. It was seen as the most recent indication of slowing global economic growth. As the world's largest consumer of metals and second-largest economy, China's economic performance is closely watched. A private survey indicated that factory activity contracted for the tenth straight month in December 2015. The Caixin China General Manufacturing PMI reported a reading below 50, specifically 49.7, signaling contraction. A PMI below 50 indicates an overall contraction of the manufacturing economy.

Bachir returned to Godolphin's British base at Newmarket and made his first appearance of the season in Europe in the Poule d'Essai des Poulains over 1600 metres at Longchamp on 14 May in which he was ridden by Dettori. Before the race, Monashee Mountain had won three straight races, including the Iowa Derby. The favorite was Cagliostro (4-5), with Stage Raider as the second choice. Bachir, at 7-2 odds, faced Prix de Fontainebleau third-place finisher Cooke Creek and Vintage Stakes winner Haatem. Cooke Creek took an early lead, with Ekraar tracking in second. Bachir regained the lead 300m out and won by four lengths over Cooke Creek, with Bosustow third and Savaglee fourth.

In 1950, the United States authorized Puerto Rico to draft its own constitution through Public Law 600, aiming to establish a republican form of government and a Bill of Rights. The Constitutional Assembly met from 1951 to 1952 to draft the document, which was approved by the U.S. Congress, creating the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

Shaarey Zedek Cemetery is a Jewish cemetery in Winnipeg, Manitoba, established in 1894 and owned by Shaarey Zedek Synagogue. The largest Jewish cemetery in the Canadian Prairies, it contains over 8,000 graves, including those of prominent rabbis and the Bronfman family. It features two ohelim (mausoleums), Commonwealth war graves, and a cenotaph honoring Jewish soldiers who died in both World Wars. In 2012, an interfaith burial section, Shaarey Shamayim Cemetery, was added. The cemetery has been the site of historic events, including a "black wedding" during the 1918 flu pandemic, and has faced vandalism in the past.

"Cookie" is a song by South Korean girl group NewJeans, released on August 1, 2022, as the third single from their debut self-titled extended play. While praised for its production and groove, the song faced controversy over alleged sexual innuendos in its lyrics, which some deemed inappropriate for a group of minors. NewJeans' agency, ADOR, denied the allegations, asserting the lyrics were intended for fans and lacked sexual connotations. The group promoted the song during their debut stage on *M Countdown*.

The magazine featured articles about dogs, and though it originally focused purely on Pedigree dogs and dog shows, it evolved to become a more general interest dog magazine. This change in focus was not without controversy. The price of CKC membership previously included a subscription to the magazine. Some members believed that if the subscription price was included in the fee, the magazine should focus on pedigree dogs and dog shows. Members who preferred this content had the option to cancel their subscription. In recent years, the magazine's editorial staff changed, and it appeared to bow to pressure from private pet owners. The magazine's focus returned to pedigree dogs and dog shows, promoting the interests of breeders in particular.

The La Salle Green Hills High School (LSGH-HS) is a Catholic secondary school that is divided into the Junior High School (Grades 7–10) and the Senior High School (Grades 11 and 12). Its Junior High School is located within the St. Brother Mutien Marie FSC building aside from the Arts and Technology classrooms which are located at the St. Benilde Gym's mezzanine, and the top floors of the St. Joseph the Worker building and Central House. Its Senior High School is located within the St. Brother Arnold Reche FSC and also houses related facilities. Previously an exclusive all-boys' school, its Senior High School opened to female students in 2020, then eventually to all levels in 2021.

It is a narrow, rarely used, scenic alternative to the Strzelecki Track, passing through red sand dunes and clay pans of the Strzelecki Desert. It is a good preparation for the longer traverse of the Simpson desert. There are gas fields, pipelines and bores for which the track is the historic access and after which it is named.

Their second single, "Back In Bloom", also produced by Ed Buller, was released in November 2011, again on Perfect Sound Forever. The song was featured in "The Barometer" by John Hall in The Independent  and in The Fly by Harriet Gibsone. Huw Stephens at BBC Radio 1, Tom Robinson at BBC Radio 6 and John Richards at KEXP played the track on their shows. They were also mentioned as one of "15 Music Artists Ready for Their Close-Up in 2012" in The Huffington Post.

The Gongala Massacre was a mass murder that occurred on September 18, 1999, in the small village of Gonagala, located in the Ampara District of Sri Lanka. According to reports, over 50 men, women and children were killed in the middle of the night. The killings are attributed to the LTTE, which is banned as a terrorist organization by a number of countries including the United States, the United Kingdom, India and the European Union.

Construction of the Armed Forces Radiobiology Research Institute's main headquarters in Bethesda, Maryland, began in 1973, and the facility was occupied in 1974. The headquarters became fully operational with the installation of a TRIGA (Training, Research, and Isotope Production General Atomics) reactor, which first achieved criticality in 1962. Key facilities at the headquarters include the reactor, laboratories for radiobiology research, and biodosimetry resources.

"L.A. Law: The Movie," also known as "L.A. Law: Return to Justice," is a 2002 drama film that serves as a reunion for the television series "L.A. Law." Directed by Michael Schultz and written by William M. Finkelstein, the film follows the dynamics at the law firm McKenzie Brackman, where new challenges arise as former partners and employees navigate personal and professional conflicts, including a high-stakes legal case involving a former client facing execution.

Philip Brandon Martin (born October 31, 1978), better known as Bishop Lamont, is an American rapper from Carson, California. He was signed to Dr. Dre's Aftermath Entertainment record label and released one project under the label, eventually leaving in 2010.

Miniclip SA is a mobile game publisher and former browser game website that launched in 2001. It was started by Robert Small and Tihan Presbie with a budget of £40,000. In 2008, the company was valued at over £275 million.

In 2009, Miniclip hosted over 400 applications on its website.

In 2015, Tencent acquired majority stakes of Miniclip. In December 2016, Miniclip crossed 1 billion downloads across its mobile games on iOS, Android, and Windows devices. In March 2022, Miniclip announced that it had reached 4 billion downloads, with 8 Ball Pool alone accounting for 1 billion.

In April 2022, Miniclip announced that it would begin prioritizing its mobile games. As a result, the browser game portal was shut down in July 2022 and the website lost all but its two most popular games, Agar.io and 8 Ball Pool.

In June 2022, Miniclip acquired SYBO, the publisher of Subway Surfers, in an undisclosed deal.

After employing a 75-pitch limit after the first month of the season, Pomeranz averaged less than 5 innings per start. On May 7, 2012, Pomeranz hit his first career home run. Pomeranz spent the 2012 season between AAA and the Rockies rotation, making 22 starts for them despite recording a 2–9 record. For the 2013 season, Pomeranz began the season in AAA and would only appear in 8 games, 4 starts for the Rockies.

While the Tsarist political police were ruthless, the Bolsheviks' Vecheka, established in 1917, used terror and violence during the civil war. This contributed to the development of the Stalinist police apparatus, which some consider an example of totalitarianism. To suppress potential opposition, Joseph Stalin increased the scope and powers of the state secret police (NKVD, GPU, KGB).

Speaking about the roadmap, First Minister Arlene Foster said: "We recognise how difficult the current restrictions are. "But those restrictions, and the determined people of Northern Ireland who have adhered to them, have saved lives and continue to do so. We don't want to keep any restriction in place any longer than we have to, but in relaxing any measure we must be cognisant of the potential effects in the transmission of the virus and our ability to save lives."

"Raban easily navigated a wealth of artistic sources and mediums, borrowing and combining ideas from East and West, fine arts and crafts from past and present. His works blended European neoclassicism, Symbolist art and Art Nouveau with oriental forms and techniques to form a distinctive visual lexicon. Versatile and productive, he lent this unique style to most artistic mediums, including the fine arts, illustration, sculpture, repousee, jewellery design, and ceramics."

The club's origins are in the merging of two separate bids during the league's expansion process in 2018, United for Macarthur and South West Sydney FC. The two entities joined forces on 20 August 2018 to create Macarthur South West United Football Club, which later that year was chosen by the FFA to join the league in the 2020–21 A-League season. Club was founded by Chairman Gino Marra and football operations Director Sam Krslovic

Spiro earned dual bachelor's degrees in Political Science and Communication Arts from the University of Wisconsin–Madison. He then attended the University of Texas at Austin, where he received a master's degree in Film Production. His early work includes film adaptations of The Convict, a short story by James Lee Burke, and The Suicide Club, a story by Robert Louis Stevenson, which starred Jonathan Pryce and Paul Bettany.

The Western Tapes, 1983 is an EP by American country rock band Lone Justice, released in 2018 by Omnivore Recordings. Recorded in 1983 at the Record Plant and Mystic Sound studios in Los Angeles, the six-track EP features early demo versions of songs later re-recorded for their 1985 debut album. Produced by Marvin Etzioni and Ryan Hedgecock, the recordings capture the band's raw energy and blend of country, rock, and punk influences. Critics praised the EP for its authenticity and Maria McKee's powerful vocals, with some noting that these versions surpassed later studio takes. The release followed *The Vaught Tapes, 1983* (2014) and preceded *Live at the Palomino, 1983* (2019).

Barry Ono (1876–1941) was a British variety theatre performer, collector of penny dreadfuls and benefactor of the British Library. His collection of songbooks was bequeathed to the British Library in 1941.

As of February 2011, the town of Belovo had a population of 60,692. Bulgarians comprised 64,334 of the Belovo Municipality, while Gypsies numbered 2,215. The provided context does not specify the number of inhabitants of Belovo Municipality with unknown ethnicity.

**NBC Nightside** was an American overnight news program that aired on NBC from November 4, 1991, to September 20, 1998. Produced by NBC News Channel in Charlotte, North Carolina, it was the network's second attempt at a late-night news show, following *NBC News Overnight* (1982–1983). Unlike its competitors, *Nightside* aired seven days a week and featured anchors who later achieved national prominence, such as Antonio Mora and Campbell Brown. Despite its profitability and decent ratings, the program was canceled in 1998.

New Zealand composers such as Gillian Whitehead and Martin Lodge have used taonga pūoro extensively in the genre of art music combining the traditional Maori instruments with western instruments. These composers were noted for this work in March 2013 by UK publication, Gramophone. In 2010, British film and orchestral composer, Paul Lewis collaborated with master taonga pūoro composer and performer, Horomona Horo, to produce, Legends of Rotorua, a fifty-minute composition for a wide variety of taonga pūoro, string quartet, harp, flute, storyteller and soprano. In 2015, Ariana Tikao and Philip Brownlee composed the first concerto for taonga puoro, called Ko Te Tātai Whetu. Tikao performed the piece with the Christchurch Symphony Orchestra, Stroma and the Nelson Symphony Orchestra, and was due to perform it with New Zealand Symphony Orchestra in April 2020 (but this was postponed due to the COVID-19 pandemic).

The Division of Sanitary Reports and Statistics was established in 1899 as part of the initial establishment of internal divisions within the Marine Hospital Service. The Division of Public Health Methods formed in 1937 within the National Institute of Health. In 1943, it merged with the Division of Sanitary Reports and Statistics, retaining the latter’s name, and was transferred to the Office of the Surgeon General.

Peter Guttman was born in Roswell, New Mexico and grew up in the Fresh Meadows neighborhood of Queens in New York City. As a five-year-old, he received early recognition of his visual skills when his artwork was exhibited at the Lever House on Manhattan's Park Avenue. By the age of twelve, Guttman received notice for engaging in medical research involving the artificial heart. He advanced ideas for the development of an internal power source, and established relationships with such pioneering doctors as Adrian Kantrowitz, Michael DeBakey and Willem Kolff, who invited Peter to spend time with him at the Cleveland Clinic.

The Yelde Hall is one of very few remaining medieval timber framed buildings in the town. It originally was divided internally for use as a market hall. Both the hall and its meeting room upstairs were used by the burgess and bailiff for a variety of meetings and trials as well as for Council meetings. The space under the Council Chamber was used as the town gaol.

Nicole Bilderback was born in South Korea and raised in Tulsa, Oklahoma, and Dallas, Texas. She was adopted through Holt International Children's Services by Lois and Jim Bilderback and has two older brothers. In junior high school, she was a cheerleader and studied dance from an early age. At 14, she enrolled at the Dallas Young Actor's Studio to pursue acting.

Wade also received criticism in 2009 for a "goodbye present" he reportedly gave to a departing IMF official after the two had dinner. "The gift, valued at $200,000, sparked widespread criticism over corruption and nepotism, with allegations that Wade was grooming his son, Karim Wade, as successor."

Brandon Teena was born in Lincoln, Nebraska. His family described him as a "tomboy." When living as a girl, he was sexually assaulted by a male relative. According to his mother, JoAnn Brandon, they sought counseling in 1991.

Churchill remained loyal to John Diefenbaker during the Progressive Conservative Party's internal quarrels of the 1960s, and worked for Diefenbaker at the party's 1967 leadership convention. "In 1967, Diefenbaker sent Churchill as an emissary to Dufferin Roblin's camp to secure support. In February 1968, Churchill attacked new leader Robert Stanfield for failing to call an election after Lester Pearson's Liberal government was unexpectedly defeated in the House."

In 2012 Gibraltar purchased two 13 m response boats from SAFE Boats International for the Royal Gibraltar Police's role in combating the rise in drug trafficking in the Strait of Gibraltar. In 2012, the Royal Gibraltar Police (RGP) Commissioner purchased two 13 m response boats from SAFE Boats International. Mr. Yome stated these vessels provide rapid response capability and improved safety for officers. Media linked the purchases to rising drug smuggling in the Mediterranean, specifically targeting increased activity off the coast of North Africa.

Dan Bejar has collaborated with Vancouver indie-rock band The New Pornographers while releasing more material as frontman of Destroyer. Known for poetic lyrics and distinctive vocals, he formed Swan Lake in 2006 with Carey Mercer (Frog Eyes) and Spencer Krug (Sunset Rubdown, Wolf Parade).

During Game 6 of the 2010 American League Championship Series against the New York Yankees, Feliz got the final three outs in the 9th inning and sent the Rangers to the World Series for the first time in franchise history. On October 30, 2010, he went three up, three down to close a 4–2 Rangers win in Game 3 of the World Series, making him the second youngest player to record a save in the World Series. The Rangers lost the World Series to the San Francisco Giants in 5 games.

As of the 2020 census there were 18,789 people, 6,059 households, and 4,310 families residing in the village. The population density was 7,835.28 inhabitants per square mile (3,025.22/km2). There were 6,757 housing units at an average density of 2,817.76 per square mile (1,087.94/km2). The racial makeup of the village was 68.35% African American, 6.67% White, 0.96% Native American, 0.72% Asian, 0.02% Pacific Islander, 15.83% from other races, and 7.45% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 26.79% of the population.

In 2015, River City Labs was able to expand into the accelerator space, dubbed RCL Accelerator, due to a partnership with the Telstra backed muru-D accelerator program. In late 2015 River City Labs announced plans to double in size. In 2017 Shoes of Prey co-founder Mike Knapp and Reload Media Founder Llew Jury were appointed joint Entrepreneurs-in-Residence (EIR)

While Carey did advocate an expanding Earth model in the mid-20th century, that model has been rejected following the recognition of plate tectonics. Adams rejects tectonics and the decades of geological and geophysical data that illustrate it. He claims that the Earth is not expanding but growing, and advocates his ideas in a DVD documentary he wrote and produced, clips of which he displays on his YouTube channel.

After five seasons with the Cowboys, new Washington Redskins owner Daniel Snyder pursued Sanders along with other high priced free agents Mark Carrier and Bruce Smith. Sanders played one season with the Redskins, having four interceptions before retiring in 2001. Late in the 2002 NFL season Sanders contemplated a return to the NFL, specifically to the Oakland Raiders. With his rights still the property of the Redskins, he lobbied and received his release from the team and was waived.

The Crimson's three NCAA Tournament appearances came at the Northeast Regional in 1980, 1983, and 1984 after it won the EIBL title in each of those seasons. The program reached regional finals in 1980 (6-3 loss to St. John's) and 1983 (4-3 loss to Maine). In 1985, Harvard shared the EIBL title with Princeton but was defeated in a playoff and did not reach the NCAA Tournament. In 1990, Nahigian's final season, Harvard participated in the first Baseball Beanpot, finishing as the runner-up to Boston College.

On February 26, 2015, Alcon Entertainment announced Ford would reprise his role as Rick Deckard in Blade Runner 2049. The film, and Ford's performance, was very well received by critics upon its release in October 2017. Scott Collura of IGN called it a, "deep, rich, smart film that's visually awesome and full of great sci-fi concepts" and Ford's role, "a quiet, sort of gut-wrenching interpretation to Deckard and what he must've gone through in the past three decades." The film grossed $259.3 million worldwide, far short of the estimated $400 million that the film needed in order to break even. In 2019, Ford had his first voice role in an animated film, as a dog named Rooster in The Secret Life of Pets 2. With filming for a fifth Indiana Jones film having been delayed by a year, Ford headlined a big-budget adaptation of Jack London's The Call of the Wild, playing prospector John Thornton. The film was released in February 2020 to a mixed critical reception but its theatrical release was shortened due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the film industry.

Appearing before the Detroit Economic Club, Immelt was joined by Michigan Governor Jennifer Granholm, Sen. Carl Levin, and Sen. Debbie Stabenow. Together they hailed the GE announcement as not only important in the development of a new technology future for the US but also important in Michigan's "comeback", a state hit the hardest by the downfall of the automotive manufacturing sector. As Granholm stated, "For a hundred years, we have been an automotive economy and we clearly need to diversify. This summer is going to be tough. Robert Ficano, then Wayne County Executive, emphasized the strategic importance of GE's arrival at the Detroit Region Aerotropolis, likening its impact to a "game-changer" for the region. Immelt noted, "Companies like GE never travel alone," highlighting that GE often attracts suppliers and partners, fostering broader economic growth. He suggested that GE's presence could create thousands of jobs and catalyze innovation. Ficano viewed the development, now known as VantagePort, as a cornerstone for revitalizing the local economy and positioning the region as a hub for advanced manufacturing and technology.

The fountain was a frequent target for vandals who would repeatedly break off fingers or hands from the sculptures. New hands were later carved and attached to the sculptures. In 1948, the fountain was temporarily removed when a streetcar underpass was built beneath Dupont Circle. When it was moved back to its original location two years later, the fountain's pumping system was replaced. When the new system was installed, workers forgot to connect the pipes to the fountain. The issue was corrected the following year and the fountain became operational.

The pixel art style was intended primarily to cut time between drawing and implementing the designs in gameplay: van den Berg found it relatively easy to create, saying that only finding the right font and designing a "rotating style" caused some issues. Although van den Berg was inspired somewhat by retro games, he liked pixel art primarily for its impressionism and that it allowed for the viewer to interpret its details for themselves. Van Dyke later said that the team never wanted to abandon the pixel art style of the previous games, declaring that it was "part of the DNA". The layout of levels was compared by van den Berg to a panorama: Both he and van Dyke said it was challenging to create mechanics that allow for player strategy while also sticking to a "one-dimensional" design; van den Berg paid specifically paid attention to forcing units to "overlap" and take the same place in a single dimension. Some weapons, such as the area of effect catapult, could take advantage of the overlap in order to hit multiple targets at once. Van den Berg felt as these elements made the game's mechanics "much less predictable".

A three-point turn is a driving maneuver used to turn a vehicle around in a narrow space, typically involving three distinct movements: driving across the road, reversing, and then driving forward again. While commonly referred to as a "three-point turn" in several countries, it is known by different names in various regions, such as "turning in the road" in the UK.

The available ethnographic evidence suggests most societies studied were patriarchal rather than matriarchal prior to Western contact. Some exhibited matrilineal or matrilocal characteristics, though these coexisted with male dominance patterns (see Patriarchy in anthropology). These findings align with sources cited elsewhere in this article, including Encyclopædia Britannica, Margaret Mead, Cynthia Eller and Steven Goldberg, whose bibliographies provided key references for this research.

Many Hmong immigrated to Michigan after the end of the Vietnam War in the 1970s. The Hmong had moved to Detroit in order to obtain employment and so members of the same families could live in the same area. Hmong people had migrated to Detroit from various places in the United States. As of that year, Metro Detroit's Hmong population is smaller than the major Hmong populations in California, Minnesota, and Wisconsin.

Salazar joined the Atlanta Braves organization in 2011 and was poised to serve as the manager of their Class A-Advanced minor league affiliate, the Lynchburg Hillcats of the Carolina League. On March 9, 2011, he was struck in the face by a foul ball hit by Braves catcher Brian McCann during a spring training game between the Braves and the St. Louis Cardinals. He was airlifted to a local hospital to undergo surgery. Salazar had his left eye removed on March 15, 2011, due to the injuries he sustained from the incident. After the injury, Salazar stated that he intended to continue working for the Braves organization, and he was expected to return to manage the Hillcats once medically cleared to return to work. Salazar returned to the Hillcats dugout on April 15, 2011.

Several neuroimaging studies have investigated theory of mind (ToM) impairments in autism spectrum disorders (ASD), particularly focusing on conditions like Asperger syndrome. The first neuroimaging study conducted on ToM in autism utilized positron emission tomography (PET) and involved a story-listening task to assess mental state recognition. This study replicated findings from adult controls, revealing that subjects with autism exhibited reduced activation in the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC). It included five patients with Asperger syndrome, and a limitation of the PET imaging was its low spatial resolution, potentially overlooking specific brain abnormalities.

The POW/MIA flag is an American flag designed as a symbol of national concern and commitment to resolving the fates of prisoners of war (POW) and those missing in action (MIA). It was created in 1972 by Newt Heisley for the National League of Families of American Prisoners and Missing in Southeast Asia during the Vietnam War. Federal law requires it to be flown at certain federal properties.

The three Trustees named in the Act met on 17 June 1830 and appointed further Trustees, bringing the total up to 23. In the weeks following the initial additions, 10 more people were appointed, bringing the total to 33 by early July 1830. Not everyone had been formally sworn in by that time. The Master of the Society of Merchant Venturers was George Hilhouse. The full names of those included were John Scandrett, George Scandrett, Henry Scandrett, and Richard Scandrett.

Colt Lyerla went undrafted in the 2014 NFL Draft, after which the Green Bay Packers signed him as an undrafted free agent in May after a rookie tryout. On August 2, 2014, during the Packers' Family Night practice, Lyerla tore his medial collateral ligament (MCL) and posterior cruciate ligament (PCL) in his right knee while attempting to hurdle a defender. Initially, the Packers waived Lyerla with the intention of placing him on injured reserve, but on August 19, 2014, they reached an injury settlement with him.

The spatial resolution in magnetic neutron radiography is limited by neutron flux and beam size. A proposed method involves scanning micro-sized neutron beams, but producing such beams is constrained to one-dimensional microbeams due to flux limitations. Current techniques are suitable for imaging simple, one-dimensional magnetic fields, where field vectors vary in strength but not direction, enabling applications in high-resolution studies of bulk materials.

The now deserted medieval village of Ditchford Frary (1066 to 1539) stood about a mile southeast of St Peter church on the Paddle Brook stream. In 1086, Ditchford Frary was held by Robert de Stafford. "The settlement included the chapel of St. Giles, recorded as part of Great Wolford parish in 1410. By the 17th century, the chapel was a ruin, and its rectory was annexed to Stretton in 1642. Earthwork evidence of the chapel remained visible in 1967."

Pynadath, whose family is Indian-American, started playing soccer at Red Star Soccer Academy in his youth. In 2013, he made headlines when Spanish giants Barcelona and Real Madrid showed interest in signing him into their youth systems. Pynadath ended up signing with Real Madrid, becoming the first American—and the first player of Indian descent—ever to join the club's youth program.

On the 19th, an F-4G fired an AGM-88 HARM at an Iraqi SAM site after a 14 nm lock-on east of Mosul. An hour later, an F-16C was fired upon by AAA but not hit. Two hours later a section of F-16C's were fired upon and dropped cluster bombs on guns north of Mosul. Iraq then later called a cease fire to celebrate Clinton's inauguration which took place on the 20th of January.

In March 2022, the patch notes of a Domina update contained a message recommending people not to wear face masks during the COVID-19 pandemic. This caused a backlash from the community and the game was review bombed on Steam, making its recent reviews rating drop to "overwhelmingly negative". Earlier patch notes contained rants about topics like pornography and Ayn Rand's "Atlas Shrugged." These were followed by statements denouncing COVID-19 mandates and transgender lifestyles. Gorissen reported several reviews as "fraudulent," but Steam moderators allowed them to remain. In August 2022, Gorissen announced a major update on Steam, mentioning the possibility of distributing Domina directly via his website for future updates.

Dan Carpenter attended Helena High School in Helena, Montana. He was a member of the National Honor Society with a 3.7 GPA and set multiple school records as a wide receiver, including the longest field goal and most touchdown receptions in a single season. During his senior season, he was selected all-state as both a kicker and punter and converted 182 point after attempts, setting a school record for the longest field goal.

The five brothers are said to have built the Llanpumsaint Parish Church around the 5th or 6th Century. It is thought that they built the church on a pre-existing pagan site. "There are five pools in Nant-cwm-cerwyn, each named after one of the saints. These pools were the destination for pilgrimages during the Middle Ages, particularly around the time of St David's Day."

According to Wincenty Kadłubek, the Germans were ambushed by Polish forces, resulting in a complete victory for Bolesław, after which King Henry withdrew from Poland. Due to the many corpses left by the battle, he noted that "dogs, devouring so many corpses of the fallen, fell into a mad ferocity, so that no one dared venture there." The site became known as "dogs' field," and the battle was part of the collective memory of the Polish nation. However, Kadłubek's account is unsubstantiated; it was already questioned in the late 19th century Encyklopedia Powszechna by Samuel Orgelbrand. Present-day historians claim this "great battle" was instead an unimportant skirmish, and the Chronica, written at the court of Bolesław's son Casimir II the Just nearly a century later, is not considered reliable on this topic.

**Military-technical cooperation**  
At the **Paris Air Show 2019** (17–23 June), held in **Le Bourget** under the patronage of the **French Aerospace Industries Association**, Azerbaijan’s **Farrukh Vezirov**, Counsellor to the Minister of Defence Industry, met with France’s **Thomas Lorne**, Head of International Cooperation at the Ministry of Defence, to discuss establishing **military-technical cooperation** between the two countries, including potential defense projects.

The Crni Kamen is a 2,536m peak in the Šar Mountains, located between Kosovo and North Macedonia. The Brezovica ski resort is located nearby.  

(Note: Changed "Republic of Macedonia" to "North Macedonia" per current naming conventions, removed subjective "famous", and tightened wording to meet word count.)

During a 1994 visit to Ethiopia, Jimmy Carter secured Prime Minister Meles Zenawi's support for an agricultural program using diammonium phosphate fertilizer and Borlaug's methods. The subsequent season saw Ethiopia achieve record harvests of major crops, with production increasing by 32% and average yields by 15% compared to the previous season. These results indicate potential for improved food production in sub-Saharan Africa.

Prosecutions have arisen from breaches of regulations concerning PPE. For example, a Leicester plastics firm, Nylacast Engineering Plastic Solutions, was fined £293,000 after a worker's death in April 2016. The company was also ordered to pay £5,300 and an additional £2,134. In another case, Gordon Fowkes, a Foundry Manager at Harrison Castings Ltd, suffered serious burns to his hands and arms from molten metal blowback in August 2007, requiring several skin grafts and a five-day hospital stay. The incident was described as easily preventable, as Fowkes was wearing rigger gloves instead of heat-resistant foundry gloves. Harrison Castings Ltd was found to have contravened regulation 12 of the Provision and Use of Work Equipment Regulations 1998 and regulation 3 of the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999.

In February 2020, the Washington Examiner reported Paul Teller would leave his post in the White House Office of Legislative Affairs to assume a new role in the Trump administration for Vice President Mike Pence. Vice President Pence appointed Teller for the role of handling and maintaining key relationships with conservative groups and lawmakers. With his new role in the Trump administration, Teller's official title in the White House was "Deputy Assistant to the President and Director of Strategic Initiatives for the Vice President"

Around 1900, Judson Moss Bemis, founder and head of the Bemis company, decided that it would be advantageous for his company to have a cotton mill in a cotton-growing area in the southern United States. The site in Madison County was selected after the county government agreed to purchase 300 acres (120 ha) near the Illinois Central rail line and transfer it to the bag company for the project. Construction began in 1900 and the first mill, with capacity of 21,000 spindles, was in operation by June of the following year.

Zanthoxylum asiaticum is used medicinally by various African peoples, including the Maasai, who employ it for malaria, cough, and influenza. Its roots contain coumarins with antiplasmodial activity, and extracts have shown antiviral efficacy against H1N1 influenza in lab tests. However, wild harvesting could impact the plant's population sustainability.

The first, was a ball hit in the hole he didn't field cleanly with the speedy Andrés Torres running. The second, was again, another crucial controversial call that went against the Braves in the series. With two on and one out and a weakly hit groundball, González elected to go to second, throwing it high, causing Omar Infante to edge up, however, the umpire ruled he came off the base.

In the morning, Israel announced that 17 individuals were killed in Gaza overnight, the first night with ground soldiers present. This brought the total to 248 killed and 1,920 injured, including some civilians. Additionally, 13 individuals surrendered to the IDF and were arrested.

Nippu Ravva (transl. Fire Spark) is a 1993 Telugu-language action film, produced by M.V.Srinivas Prasad on Yuvarathna Arts banner and directed by A. Kodandarami Reddy. The film stars Nandamuri Balakrishna, Vijayashanti, and music composed by Bappi Lahiri & Raj–Koti with A. R. Rahman's background score. Nippu Ravva and Bangaru Bullodu, also starring Balakrishna, were released on the same day and both ran for over 100 days. It was dubbed into Tamil and Hindi as Suriya and Mawaali Raaj, respectively.
On August 25, 2013, Dan Carpenter signed with the Arizona Cardinals to compete for the starting placekicker job against incumbent Jay Feely, but was cut two days later.
Tim Hauck (born December 20, 1966) is a former American football safety who played 13 seasons in the NFL and later became a coach. He won Super Bowl LII as safeties coach for the Philadelphia Eagles.

Warshades are a fusion between a human and a scientifically altered Kheldian known as a Nictus. Unlike most Nictus, which forcibly take over host bodies, Warshades have willing hosts. Warshades have two power sets similar to Peacebringers but are uniquely different, lacking melee attacks and instead using control-type powers. Their transformations include Dark Nova, granting Blaster-like abilities, and Black Dwarf, providing Tanker-like abilities.

As of 2005, $50 billions (USD) have been invested in the PRC by Taiwanese companies, and about 1 million Taiwanese live in the PRC, often to run their own businesses. Although this is generally good for the economy of Taiwan, some Taiwanese have expressed worries about this situation, arguing that the island has become increasingly dependent on the PRC economy. Others believe it is a positive thing, because it would make any military intervention by the PRC against Taiwan very costly, and therefore less probable.

Prakash Nanjappa (born 29 February 1976) is an Indian shooter known for competing in the 10 metre air pistol and 50 metre pistol events. He began shooting in 1999 and gained prominence after winning a bronze medal at the 2013 ISSF World Cup in Changwon. Nanjappa also secured a silver medal at the 2014 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. He qualified for the 2016 Rio Olympics, finishing 25th in the qualification round. Nanjappa is the son of national-level shooter P. N. Papanna.

Retaj Al Rayyan Mecca is a 4-star hotel located in Mecca, Saudi Arabia. The hotel has 22 stories and 670 rooms and suites as well as a restaurant, fitness center, and meeting rooms. A report published by Trade Arabia stated that the Al Rayyan Mecca hotel constitutes the Retaj Group's first step towards its “expansion plan in the GCC, particularly in Saudi Arabia.”

Carnovsky was born in St. Louis, Missouri on September 5, 1897 to Ike and Jennie Carnovsky, both Russian Jewish immigrants. His father, a grocer, took him to performances of the Yiddish theater. In 1975 he recalled: "There was such richness in their portrayals of Jewish life. I could savor it. Once I smelled greasepaint, I was committed." He graduated from Washington University in 1920 and then moved to Boston where he acted in his first professional stage production.

Valmiera (pronunciation; Lang-de; Lang-pl see other names) is the largest city of the historical Vidzeme region, Latvia, with a total area of 19.35 square kilometres (7 square miles). As of 2002, Valmiera had a population of 27,323, and in 2020 – 24 879. It is a state city the seat of Valmiera Municipality.

Valmiera lies at the crossroads of several important roads, 107 kilometres (66 miles) to the north-east from Riga, the capital of Latvia, and 50 km (31 mi) south of the border with Estonia. Valmiera lies on both banks of the Gauja River.

Alongside the series order announcement, it was announced that Melissa Barrera and Veronica Osorio had been cast as Lyn and Emma, respectively. On November 14, 2017, Ser Anzoategui, Chelsea Rendon, Carlos Miranda, and Maria Elena Laas were announced as having joined the series' main cast. It was also announced that Mishel Prada had replaced Veronica Osorio in the role of Emma. On December 8, 2017, the final additions to the cast were announced. These consisted of the castings of Elena Campbell-Martínez, Ramsés Jiménez, Luis Bordonada, Elizabeth De Razzo, Renee Victor, Adelina Anthony, and Erika Soto in recurring roles.

In 2009, Jon Morter and his wife Tracy launched a campaign to prevent X Factor winner Joe McElderry from reaching the #1 spot on the UK charts. They aimed to stop McElderry's cover of Miley Cyrus's "The Climb" from becoming the top single by promoting Rage Against the Machine's "Killing In The Name" instead. The Morters established a Facebook account titled "Rage Against the Machine for Christmas No. 1" to rally support for their cause. Morter explained his understanding of how the charts worked to The Guardian, having previously learned from his attempt to boost Rick Astley's "Never Gonna Give You Up" in 2008. He described the scale of the 2009 campaign as having "gone stratospheric" compared to the previous year's efforts.

Patini struggled financially as a painter. He developed cardiac ailments in later decades. Despite financial struggles, Patini painted sacred subjects, including *The Crucifix* for San Pelino basilica near Corfinio, *Il Purgatorio o Redenzione*, and *St Carlo Borromeo among the plague victims*. Though holding anticlerical views, he created works for San Demetrio ne’ Vestini in Calascio, the Madonna della Libera Sanctuary in Pratola Peligna, and L’Aquila’s Duomo and Santa Maria della Concezione. His oeuvre blended genre scenes with religious themes like *The Guardian Angel* and *L’Immacolata e Santi*.
Everts is known for his smooth riding style, making everything appear effortless. He frequently rides in a standing position, even in tight corners, where his cornering control is highly regarded.

On 20 November, the Constitutional Court ruled that a proposed amendment to the 2007 constitution was invalid. The amendment would have transformed the Senate from a partially appointed body to a fully elected body. The Pheu Thai Party rejected the ruling, claiming that the court had no jurisdiction over the case. Anti-government protest numbers swelled over the following weekend, while the Red Shirts, this time in support of the government, also staged counter-protests. On 25 November, anti-government protestors led by Suthep began surrounding and forcing their way into several government offices, forcing their closure. The protests was largely non-violent, until clashes between anti- and pro-government groups on 30 November and 1 December resulted in 4 deaths and 57 wounded. Escalation of protests on 1 December saw two days of clashes between anti-government protesters and the police, who used tear gas and water cannons to halt the protesters as they attempted to force their way into Government House—119 injured people were reported. On 3 December, the police removed their barricades and allowed protesters to enter the site, sharply reducing tensions as part of a temporary truce, in order to allow both sides to celebrate the King's Birthday; although, protest leaders said they would continue rallying until their demands to remove "Thaksinocracy" from Thai politics were met.

Sean Flint was critical of the name choice, saying "it describes absolutely nothing about it other than the fact that it may contain ducks". Opinions on the game's learning curve were mixed; Chris Compendio found the fast-paced multiplayer mode and a lack of helpful tutorials to be disorienting. Several reviewers praised Duck Game's frenetic multiplayer, with Steven Hanson highlighting its humor and weapon mastery, though noting the need to remember controls across its 4-button layout. Critics found the level design inconsistent and the single-player experience lacking depth compared to titles like *Towerfall*, with some lamenting the absence of a cohesive campaign or progressive challenge.

Sant Hirdaram Sahib, also known as Baba Hariram, was born on September 21, 1906, in the village of Bhangun, in the Nawabshah district of Sindh, Pakistan. His birth name was Ramchand, the son of Indumati and Shri Sahajram Ji. Ramchand became a disciple of Baba Kriparam, who named him Hirdharam. In 1948, Sant Hirdaram Sahib migrated to India and settled in Jodhpur, later moving to Ajmer and then to Pushkar. The flamelet concept considers the turbulent flame as an aggregate of thin, laminar (Re < 2000), locally one-dimensional flamelet structures present within the turbulent flow field. Counterflow diffusion flame is a common laminar flame which is used to represent a flamelet in a turbulent flow. Its geometry consists of opposed and axi-symmetric fuel and oxidizer jets. As the distance between the jets is decreased and/or the velocity of the jets is increased, the flame is strained and departs from its chemical equilibrium until it eventually extinguishes. The mass fraction of species and temperature fields can be measured or calculated in laminar counterflow diffusion flame experiments. When calculated, a self-similar solution exists, and the governing equations can be simplified to only one dimension i.e. along the axis of the fuel and oxidizer jets. It is in this direction where complex chemistry calculations can be performed affordably.

In mathematics and computer programming, exponentiating by squaring is a general method for fast computation of large positive integer powers of a number, or more generally of an element of a semigroup, like a polynomial or a square matrix. Some variants are commonly referred to as square-and-multiply algorithms or binary exponentiation. These can be of quite general use, for example in modular arithmetic or powering of matrices. For semigroups for which additive notation is commonly used, like elliptic curves used in cryptography, this method is also referred to as double-and-add.

Obscurantism is the practice of deliberately preventing the facts or full details of a matter from becoming known. An obscurantist may oppose certain forms of social reform or intellectual movements.

The Alliance française is a global network of French language and cultural centers founded in Paris in 1883 by prominent figures including Louis Pasteur, Ferdinand de Lesseps, and Jules Verne. With over 440,000 students worldwide, it promotes French language education through independently run local franchises. The Paris headquarters, Alliance française Paris Île-de-France, is the oldest and largest branch, hosting thousands of international students annually. While primarily funded by course fees, it receives partial government support. The organization collaborates with ministries, businesses, and cultural institutions to advance French language learning.

The VENUE sound system is a series of digital mixing consoles developed by Avid Technology, designed for live sound applications. The range includes several models, starting with the D-Show, the first and largest control surface, which was discontinued in 2015. The Profile model became a standard in the industry for touring and festivals, while the SC48 offered an all-in-one solution with built-in processing and I/O capabilities. The S3L-X, introduced in 2013, featured a compact design and modular architecture, allowing for extensive connectivity and integration with digital audio workstations. The flagship S6L, released in 2015, replaced earlier models and introduced advanced features such as multiple touch screens, enhanced processing power, and support for high-performance audio paths. All VENUE consoles are compatible with Pro Tools for direct recording and offer inter-compatibility of show files, although some adjustments may be necessary due to architectural differences.

Dr. William Baer, an orthopedic surgeon at Johns Hopkins during the late 1920s, used maggot therapy to treat a series of patients with osteomyelitis and published his findings. Dr. Baer first got the idea when, during World War I, he treated two soldiers with broken femurs who had lain on the ground for seven days without food and water. He noticed that neither man had a fever or signs of sepsis, and upon removing their clothing, he found the wounds filled with thousands of maggots, likely those of the blow fly. Initially surprised and disgusted, he observed that the wounds were filled with "beautiful pink granulation tissue" and were healing well.

Skimlinks is a content monetization platform for online publishers (including editorial sites, forums, bloggers, social networks, and app developers). It specializes in technology that automatically affiliates product links from publishers' commerce content.

The constellation Cetus lies far from the Milky Way's galactic plane, reducing interstellar dust and enhancing visibility of distant galaxies. M77 hosts an active supermassive black hole. The cluster JKCS 041, among the most distant known, provides insights into early galaxy evolution due to its ancient quiescent population.
